title: Knicks Owner Cancels Watch Party As Spat With Mamdani Escalates
description: Hours before Wednesday’s game began, James Dolan, the owner of the Knicks, and Mayor Zohran Mamdani were publicly battling over who was to blame for the cancellation.
author: Santul Nerkar and Maia Coleman

title: Pope Leo Is Blessing the Sagrada Familia in Barcelona. To Some, the Church Is a Curse.
description: The pontiff celebrated a Mass in Antoni Gaudí’s iconic, unfinished basilica during his Spain trip. But for some Barcelona residents, completing the construction could mean knocking down their homes.
author: Jason Horowitz

title: Trump Shrugs Off High Prices Amid Iran War and Says ‘I Love the Inflation’
description: As inflation outpaces workers’ wages, the Trump administration insists that its agenda is working.
author: Tony Romm

title: Trump Says He’s ‘Not Looking to Renew’ Canada-Mexico Trade Deal
description: The president’s comments come at a crucial moment in talks among the three countries over the renewal of their free-trade agreement.
author: Matina Stevis-Gridneff, Ana Swanson and Tony Romm

title: SpaceX’s IPO Could Turn 4,400 Employees Into Millionaires
description: While Elon Musk may soon become a trillionaire, his rocket company’s market debut is set to the change the lives of its current and former employees, too.
author: Kirsten Grind

title: Inside Trump’s White House, the Epstein Files Caused a Freakout
description: The president’s top advisers gathered in a series of Situation Room meetings as they struggled to contain a scandal engulfing Donald Trump himself.
author: Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan

title: Why the Real A.I. Threat Is in the Back Office
description: As artificial intelligence spreads, millions of middle-class jobs in human resources, billing and payroll could be at risk. Most are held by women.
author: Ben Casselman

title: California’s Excuses Are Damaging Faith in Government
description: Taking a week to count votes is doing it wrong.
author: The Editorial Board

title: With Bill Pulte, Trump Is Sending a Dangerous Message
description: President Trump’s decision to place Bill Pulte at the head of the U.S. intelligence community is not just another poor personnel choice. It’s a warning.
author: John Sipher

title: John Basinger, Who Memorized All 12 Books of ‘Paradise Lost,’ Dies at 92
description: After nearly nine years of practice, he made John Milton’s epic poem vividly dramatic for audiences and inspired a study of his “memory virtuosity.”
author: Alex Traub

title: On the Eve of the World Cup, U.S. Immigration Policy Turns Some Away
description: Some fans and participants hoping to enter the United States for the World Cup have complained that restrictive immigration rules have presented a roadblock.
author: Tariq Panja

title: You Love Your Native Garden. But Will Buyers Love It Too?
description: For homeowners who have ‘rewilded’ their gardens into mini nature preserves, a moment of truth arrives when they prepare to sell.
author: Jessie Graham

title: The Supreme Court Doesn’t Own the Constitution
description: It starts with “We the people” for a reason.
author: Jamelle Bouie

title: Steve Hilton Wins Second Spot in California Governor Race, Beating Tom Steyer
description: Steve Hilton, a Republican endorsed by President Trump, will face Xavier Becerra, a Democrat, in the general election. Tom Steyer, another Democrat, will not advance.
author: Laurel Rosenhall

title: Navy Admiral Fired by Hegseth Advances to Runoff to Replace Mace
description: Nancy Lacore would face a tough fall election in a district drawn in 2021 to be more Republican. She is seeking to replace Representative Nancy Mace, who ran unsuccessfully for governor instead of running for re-election.
author: Nick Corasaniti and Bayliss Wagner

title: For America’s 250th, Let’s Get Something Clear About Our National Identity
description: It depends on both ideas and cultural inheritance.
author: Ross Douthat

title: Everybody Is a Loser in This Middle East War
description: None of the participants in the recent wars of the Middle East can claim victory. All are to blame.
author: Thomas L. Friedman

title: F.D.A. Clears Sunscreen Ingredient Long Used in Europe and Asia
description: Dermatologists say bemotrizinol offers advantages over other compounds in sunscreens currently on the market.
author: Dani Blum

title: Social Security Benefits Could Be Cut in 6 Years Unless Congress Acts
description: The trust fund for the program, which supports roughly 68 million Americans, is on schedule to be depleted in the next six years. Benefits could be cut on average by 22 percent.
author: Tara Siegel Bernard and Margot Sanger-Katz

title: She’s Set to Swim the Entire 900-Mile California Coast — Sharks Permitting
description: Swimming 900 miles won’t be “the worst way to spend four months,” Catherine Breed says — as long as the great whites, jellyfish and elephant seals leave her alone.
author: Nick Romeo and Gabriela Bhaskar
